chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1479 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For katcha peshi. 07.11.2016. Mr. Heman Das Kolhi, Advocate alongwith petitioner. Mr. Sher Muhammad Leghari, State Counsel a/w Inspector Mubarak Ali SHO P.S Chhachhro, District Tharparkar and Naseer Ahmed Jogi DEO (Primary) District Tharparkar. Respondents No.4 is present in person. = Today, the police officials have filed their statement, in which they have denied the allegations of harassment made by the petitioner. However, they undertake to provide protection to the petitioner as he is doing a noble work for the community of voluntarily teaching the students of primary level. Today, Mr. Naseer Ahmed Jogi, DEO Tharparkar, who is respondent No.7 is present and submits that school is running there and no one is allowed to cause any harassment to the teachers as well as present petitioner. Today, private respondent No.4-Sharif is also present and submits that he has never caused any harassment to the petitioner, who is also present in person. Be that as it may, respondents No.6 & 7, who belong to Ministry of Education, are directed to ensure that school is being run in an utmost beneficial manner to its students and the area community and should seek police assistance whenever there is a complaint of any harassment caused by anyone to the petitioner or any other school teacher. In this regard, police officials/respondents are also directed to provide requisite protection to the petitioner, if he approaches them with such a request but strictly in accordance with law.

Be that as it may, respondents No.6 & 7, who belong to Ministry of Education, are directed to ensure that school is being run in an utmost beneficial manner to its students and the area community and should seek police assistance whenever there is a complaint of any harassment caused by anyone to the petitioner or any other school teacher. In this regard, police officials/respondents are also directed to provide requisite protection to the petitioner, if he approaches them with such a request but strictly in accordance with law. With these observations, the present petition stands disposed of. JUDGE Shahid
